mechanism_narrative: Federal funds rate at or near zero (FEDFUNDS ≤ 0.25%) signals maximum monetary accommodation. Under this regime, equity valuations expand as the discount rate applied to future corporate earnings declines, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 20-day returns in more than 40% of instances when FEDFUNDS ≤ 0.25%, or Rank IC < 0.02 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month payroll employment growth (PAYEMS) above 500K persons signals robust labor market momentum and consumer spending capacity. This drives positive equity returns over 60 trading days as forward earnings expectations rise.
failure_condition: SPX 60-day forward returns show no significant positive correlation with PAYEMS growth (Spearman ρ < 0.15 over any 3-year window), or mechanism fails to predict direction in > 55% of test periods.

mechanism_narrative: Retail sales (RSAFS) and housing starts (HOUST) jointly signal consumer and construction demand strength. When both series show month-over-month growth simultaneously, forward equity returns are positive over 20 days due to improved earnings visibility and reduced recession risk.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS and HOUST both show positive month-over-month growth
evidence_refs: fact:RSAFS at 535982M and HOUST at 1496K as of 2020-07-15
failure_condition: Joint positive growth in RSAFS and HOUST fails to predict positive 20-day SPX returns in > 45% of triggered instances, or interaction term coefficient is not statistically significant (p > 0.10) in logistic regression.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production (INDPRO) declining month-over-month signals supply-side weakness and potential margin compression. This produces negative S&P 500 forward returns over 5 trading days as investors reprrice earnings expectations downward.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5-day returns in more than 50% of instances when INDPRO declines > 0.5%, or Rank IC < 0.01 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Unemployment rate (UNRATE) rising above 8% signals labor market deterioration and reduced consumer purchasing power. This produces negative S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days as earnings forecasts are revised downward.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in more than 55% of instances when UNRATE > 8.0%, or Rank IC < 0.02 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Consumer price inflation (CPIAUCSL) accelerating above 2.5% year-over-year while FEDFUNDS remains below 1% signals monetary policy lag. This creates a negative real rate environment that initially supports equities but triggers volatility and negative 60-day returns as rate hike expectations build.
evidence_refs: fact:CPIAUCSL at 259.681 and FEDFUNDS at 0.1% as of 2020-08-15
regime_hint: monetary policy lag with inflation acceleration
failure_condition: SPX 60-day forward returns are positive in more than 50% of triggered instances, or the interaction coefficient between CPIAUCSL and FEDFUNDS is not statistically significant (p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: Real GDP growth (GDPC1) measured quarterly shows positive acceleration when compared to prior quarter. This signals sustained economic expansion and drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over 60 trading days as corporate earnings growth accelerates.
failure_condition: SPX 60-day forward returns show no significant positive correlation with GDPC1 growth (Spearman ρ < 0.12 over any 3-year window), or mechanism fails to predict direction in > 52% of test periods.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production (INDPRO) growth combined with declining unemployment (UNRATE) signals a tight labor market and supply-constrained expansion. This regime produces negative equity returns over 20 trading days as investors anticipate wage inflation and margin compression.
evidence_refs: fact:INDPRO at 101.3605 and UNRATE at 8.4% as of 2020-08-15
regime_hint: late-cycle supply constraint with tight labor
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in more than 55% of triggered instances, or the interaction term coefficient between INDPRO and UNRATE is not statistically significant (p > 0.10) in logistic regression.
